name: Ambila Chicken
recipeIngredient: 15 fresh hot red cherry peppers (or 8 red chilies)
1 large lemongrass stalk, or 4 short ones
1 pound Chinese long beans (or green beans), trimmed and cut into 2-inch pieces
1/3 cup corn oil or safflower oil
3 pounds chicken, cut into 16 serving pieces (about 3 to 4 inches)
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the seasoning paste: Wear rubber gloves and seed and devein the jalapeños. For a moderately hot dish, use about half of the cherry peppers or red chilies.
2. Trim the root ends and tough tops of lemongrass, discarding dry outer leaves. Slice the lemongrass thinly.
3. Cut gingerroot into 4 pieces.
4. Process all ingredients in a food processor until a smooth paste forms.
5. Heat oil in a heavy kettle over moderately low heat until hot.
6. Cook the seasoning paste, stirring frequently, for about 25 minutes or until dry and very fragrant.
7. Add chicken and cook over moderate heat, stirring, for an additional 10 minutes.
8. Stir in water and simmer the chicken covered, stirring occasionally, for 25 minutes or until almost cooked through. Skim any fat from the surface.
9. Add beans and simmer, covered, until beans are almost tender (5 to 10 minutes).
10. Stir in lemon juice, soy sauce, sugar, and salt. Simmer, covered, for an additional 5 minutes or until beans are tender.
(Braised Chicken with Chilies and Chinese Long Beans)
Notes: The flavorful seasoning paste can be used to season chicken, beef, lamb, or seafood.
Serve the braised chicken with rice.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.
